answer:
talking u out of it. mostly.

verdict: dont do full event sourcing w/ team of 4 building billing v1. do append-only ledger instead — gets u 80% of benefit w/ 20% of pain

why not ES:
- ES tax is real: projections, replays, schema/event versioning, snapshotting, eventual consistency btwn write + read models. thats infra work, not billing work
- 4 ppl = ~2-3 actually coding after mtgs/ops. ur burn rate goes to plumbing not features
- billing bugs r bad enough w/o "projection lagged" or "replay broke after event v3 rename" incidents
- usage-based charges = high event volume. replaying millions of usage events to rebuild state is slow + u end up building snapshots/compaction early = more complexity
- hiring/onboarding harder, debugging harder, tooling immature vs boring crud+ledger

what ES actually buys u: audit trail, temporal queries ("what was balance on may 3"), retroactive corrections. u can get ALL of that cheaper:

do this instead:
1. immutable double-entry ledger table (never update/delete rows, corrections = new offsetting entries). this is the industry std for billing — stripe works this way internally
2. usage events go in append-only table (or kafka/queue) + aggregate into rating/invoicing jobs
3. normal relational models for subs/customers, w/ audit log table if u want history

caveats where id flip:
- if teammates have shipped ES in prod b4 + u expect complex retroactive repricing/backdating as core feature, ES defensible
- even then: ES the usage metering pipeline only, keep subscription mgmt boring crud

also srsly consider not building at all: stripe billing, lago (open src), metronome/orb for usage-based. team of 4 building billing from scratch in 2026 needs strong reason (weird pricing model, cost at huge scale, compliance)

tldr: ledger + append-only usage events = auditability u want. ES = complexity tax u dont need. + eval buy-vs-build 1st
